scope: healthcheck/heartbeat/watchdog/alarm cohort frontmatter and retrieval-readiness audit
learner_facing_quality_improvement: This wave narrows learner confusion around keepalive, heartbeat, and healthcheck questions by isolating a non-Pilot cohort that is already lint-clean and retrieval-active, so the next metadata migration can improve first-hit routing without touching the Pilot baseline.

verification.lint_probe_result: all three sampled non-Pilot docs pass the current authoring lint because they still start with an H1; there is no Pilot conflict and no frontmatter/H1 collision yet in this cohort
verification.retrieval_probe_result: the keepalive primer already appears in no-rerank drift analysis and the transaction-heartbeat/websocket-heartbeat docs already appear in v22 cohort-eval artifacts, confirming that metadata coverage is the current bottleneck

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: keepalive랑 heartbeat 차이 뭐예요, 왜 keepalive인데 끊겨요, idle timeout이랑 heartbeat 헷갈려요
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is the only beginner entrypoint in this cohort and therefore the best first-hit fix for learner confusion.

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: load balancer healthcheck 왜 실패해요, readiness/liveness 차이, 서버는 살았는데 트래픽이 안 가요
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is the cleanest bridge from load-balancer basics into concrete ops symptoms without any Pilot overlap.

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: long running job heartbeat lock, transaction heartbeat 뭐예요, 배치 살아있음 신호랑 락은 왜 달라요
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It already shows up in evaluation artifacts, so migrating it would turn an existing retrieval signal into a canonical v3 node.

guardrails: No Pilot-locked corpus file was modified.
No out-of-scope script change was attempted in this lane.
No corpus doc was edited because this QA wave only recorded retrieval-readiness for a safe non-Pilot cohort.
